page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n4 family. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel CPU cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and AVX-512.

Benchmark data reveals a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU test of 1564.68 and Geekbench single-core score of 1868.0, fall within the average range. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics consistently register in the bottom 25%. The stress-ng multi-core performance of 3219.87 and Geekbench multi-core score of 4154.0 are weak, as is the memory bandwidth for large block reads at 33.17 GB/sec and writes at 21.19 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations at 4.93 million operations per second perform in the lower tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-4 offers a high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel CPU processing. However, the tradeoff is limited multi-threaded compute scaling and lower memory bandwidth. It is best suited for workloads such as moderate database operations, single-threaded text processing, static web serving, which achieved an average throughput of 7.0 GB/sec, and lightweight LLM inference tasks using small models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 2 physical cores. It provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1868.0, but weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads with low CPU concurrency demands. It is best suited for static web serving, moderate database operations, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It features Intel AMX instructions, average single-core performance, and weak multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 instance offering 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, Intel AMX support, and average single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual instance featuring Intel AMX instructions, tailored for memory-bound workloads with moderate single-threaded processing requirements.
bullet_points: Configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM on an Intel x86_64 architecture
Features 2 physical cores with dedicated allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling
Well-suited for static web serving, lightweight LLM inference, and memory-bound tasks
